item.title: Adani Power Drives Group Shares Rebound as Heat Wave Boosts India Energy Demand
item.description: Adani group companies are closing in on a combined market value of $200 billion.

item.title: Byju’s Founder Sentenced to Six Months for Contempt of Court
item.description: The founder of failed Indian technology firm Byju’s was sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Base Metals Advance as US Touts Progress Toward Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Copper climbed and aluminum was on track for its highest close in four years on cautious optimism that the US and Iran could reach a peace deal, despite a fresh flare-up of hostilities in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: RBNZ’s Split Vote to Hold Sends Kiwi Higher on Rate Hike Bets
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank kept interest rates unchanged in a split decision that was only resolved by the Governor’s casting vote.

item.title: BOJ’s Ueda Warns of Oil Shock Impact on Entire Inflation Regime
item.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda indicated the need for vigilance over the impact of oil price spikes on the underlying inflation trend without dropping a clear hint on how that dynamic might influence the outcome at next month’s policy meeting.

item.title: China Bond Bears Eye Niche Swap Trade as Rally Doubts Grow
item.description: Chinese investors are betting a months-long rally in two-year government bonds has gone too far, turning to arbitrage trades more commonly used in the US and Europe to put that view to work.

item.title: China’s Industrial Profits Soar at Fastest in Over Two Years
item.description: China’s industrial companies saw their profits soar at the fastest in more than two years, lifted higher by demand for artificial intelligence-related goods and a surge in oil prices stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: Australia’s Core Inflation Accelerates in Challenge for RBA
item.description: Australia’s core inflation remained above the top of the Reserve Bank’s target in April, complicating policymakers’ task as signs emerge that rapid fire interest-rate hikes have begun to weigh on economic activity.

item.title: Samsung Chip Workers Accept Wage Deal That Staves Off Strike
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s largest union voted in favor of a compensation deal that will hand chip workers an average bonus of about $340,000, averting a strike that threatened to disrupt global chip supply.

item.title: Astellas Pharma Shares Fall After Unveiling Five-Year Plan
item.description: Astellas Pharma Inc. shares fell to the lowest in about four months after the Tokyo-based drugmaker’s five-year plan offered few surprises.

item.title: EU Firms Warm to China Even as Tensions Spiral Over Export Surge
item.description: European firms in China are turning more upbeat about their business outlook, a new survey found, just as Brussels appears poised to take action against a swelling trade imbalance with Beijing.

item.title: UBS’ Khan Says AI Will Impact Jobs While Aiding Productivity
item.description: UBS Group AG’s Asia Pacific President Iqbal Khan said artificial intelligence will free up capacity and improve productivity but also have an impact on jobs.

item.title: SQM Boosts Lithium Guidance as Earnings Top Estimates
item.description: SQM reported a sharp increase in first-quarter profit and raised its lithium sales forecast, betting that robust demand from battery storage systems will keep the market tightly supplied.

item.title: Adnoc Exports Another LNG Shipment Through Hormuz to India
item.description: Another tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has exited the Strait of Hormuz, adding to a recent uptick in energy flows through the vital waterway.

item.title: SK Hynix Joins $1 Trillion Club on AI Memory Chip Dominance
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. has surged past a $1 trillion market valuation after its shares soared more than 900% in the past year, reflecting investor confidence in its position as the leading supplier of advanced memory chips powering artificial intelligence.

item.title: Fireworks AI in Talks for Funding at $15 Billion Valuation
item.description: Fireworks AI, a startup that helps companies run artificial intelligence models, is in talks to raise a new round of funding that would value the company at $15 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Gold Holds Loss as Traders Weigh Prospects for US-Iran Deal
item.description: Gold held a loss, as fresh clashes in the Persian Gulf weighed on hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

item.title: Court Ruling Ends Institutional Crisis at Colombian Central Bank
item.description: A Colombian court suspended a rule that required the finance minister to be present at central bank meetings, ending an institutional crisis that gave the government an effective veto over interest rate decisions.

item.title: SEC Chairman Eyes ‘Gun-Jumping’ Rule Changes to Spur More IPOs
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission is mulling changes to decades-old rules that prohibit companies from certain communications during the going-public process, as the Wall Street regulator looks to boost initial public offerings.

item.title: Trump Backs CFTC as Prediction Market Regulator Amid State Fight
item.description: President Donald Trump backed the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as the sole regulator of prediction markets, siding with the agency amid a growing legal fight with states over the multibillion-dollar industry.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Gets Another Greenlight Toward Faster Index Inclusion
item.description: FTSE Russell adopted a rule change that will speed the addition of newly listed large-cap companies to its main indexes, weeks ahead of SpaceX’s expected record-breaking initial public offering.

item.title: US Says Process to Form China Board of Trade to Start Shortly
item.description: US Trade Ambassador Jamieson Greer said his agency would issue a notice in the Federal Register “shortly” to seek public comments on a Board of Trade with China, a formal step to establish a new mechanism to manage economic relations between the two nations.

item.title: Slim to Invest $5 Billion This Year, Won’t Seek New Pemex Bets
item.description: Mexican billionaire Carlos Slim is planning to invest around $5 billion this year but will steer clear of new ventures with oil driller Petroleos Mexicanos, the 86-year-old chairman emeritus of Grupo Carso said Tuesday.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Rise as Iran Deal Hopes Hold: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to edge higher Wednesday in a sign traders are clinging to expectations the US and Iran will sign a peace deal even as military strikes in the Persian Gulf persisted.

item.title: Oil Steadies as US Sees Progress in Iran Deal Despite Tensions
item.description: Oil was little changed in early Asian trading, as the US touted progress toward a peace deal with Iran despite fresh hostilities and uncertainty over the vital Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Mubadala Offer $1.91 Billion GlobalFoundries Share Block
item.description: Mubadala Investment Co., Abu Dhabi’s sovereign wealth fund, is seeking to raise $1.91 billion from an unregistered block sale of GlobalFoundries Inc. shares, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Kardigan Files for IPO to Fund Cardiovascular Disease Treatment
item.description: Kardigan Inc. filed for a US initial public offering that will help fund three late-stage drugs that target the root cause of cardiovascular diseases.

item.title: Bank of Korea Seen Favoring Hawkish Hold as Inflation Risks Grow
item.description: South Korea’s central bank is widely expected to keep its policy rate unchanged this week, with investors watching for signs policymakers are formally shifting to a hawkish stance as inflation risks intensify and growth proves more resilient than expected.

item.title: What Trump’s Venezuela Blueprint Means for Cuba
item.description: Can Cuba withstand mounting US pressure? On the Big Take: the campaign to cripple Cuba’s government and why US intervention in Venezuela may be a blueprint for Havana’s future.

item.title: Uruguay Extends Monetary Pause, Holding Key Rate at 5.75%
item.description: Uruguay’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 5.75% for a second consecutive policy meeting while also warning that the conflict in the Middle East has increased the upside risk to inflation.
